text.title: 5 days in Bangkok Thailand 🇹🇭 | Cafes hopping, Vintage Shops, Temples & Street Food | Travel Vlog
text.u: this week we’re in Bangkok
we’ll be spending the next 5 days here
there’s so many cool shops and cafes I want to check out
this is a vintage-inspired café and art space
with a small store out front
the cafe is in this 200-year-old renovated shophouse
I love how they preserved so much of the original building
and and all the mix-matching interior
they also have a lovely outdoor sitting area
it was a bit hot outside, so we decided to sit inside
a cold brew is so refreshing on a humid day
we’re also getting lunch here
I wasn’t expecting much from the food but everything was delicious
I would come back here just for the food
right across from the cafe is this art street
it was nice to see artworks painted by local schools
I also just enjoyed the streetscape of the city
it was starting to rain pretty heavily
e’ll spend today just shopping around
this whole area has so many unique stores
this is an antique and ceramic shop
but it sells a mix of everything
the space itself is full of character
and everything is so thoughtfully curated
I love finding unique pieces like this when I travel
I think I spent almost 30 minutes looking through everything
you can definitely find such cute ceramics
highly recommend a visit if you’re looking for cute and unique souvenirs
it was sunny by the time I finished
Song Wat Road is full of shops and cafes
so you can just wander around the alleyways
this was the cutest little shop
it had a mermaid, sea-themed concept
and was selling all types of knick knacks, home goods and fashion accessories
the shop itself was so whimsical
so many shell and pearl designs
but this might have been my favorite store
they had so many cute handmade ceramics
these ones are actually so cool
the shop also hosts ceramics workshops
which would also make such a nice souvenir
even found a kiki bowl
there’s also a lot of fragrance stores here
but this one was my favorite
they had so many unique scents
I especially loved this one
this is our last shop for today
they had a bunch of second-hand compact cameras
as well as vintage ceramics and home decorations
it’s also connected to this vinyl record store
they had a lot of oldies
taking a grab to our first stop for today
unlike Korea and Japan, I was a bit worried about navigating my way around an unfamiliar city
so I wanted to be as well connected to the internet as possible
for this trip I decided to use e-sim from Simify
which is a digital sim so you don’t have to switch out your sim card just install it when you land
from booking grabs, finding directions and looking up cute cafes
having an e-sim ready made moving around the city so much easier
I also really liked that they had bundles so I could buy a sim covering multiple countries
if you have travel plans coming up you can use my code SOL15 to get 15% off your purchase :)
our cafe of choice today is this Australian inspired cafe
they have proper Australian breakfast and coffee
with some Thai inspired flavors
I also got a latte with tiramisu dark roast beans
the coffee was deliciously chocolatey and smooth
the spicy Tom Yum flavour went really well with the creamy sauce
we’re at Wat Pho temple
which is the oldest temple in Bangkok
the structures themselves were so impressive
covered in these beautiful floral ceramics
I loved seeing all the vibrant colours and patterns
but the temple is most famous for its reclining Buddha statue
which is 46 metres long and 15 metres high
the soles of the Buddha’s feet are inlaid with mother-of-pearl symbolic patterns
one thing I love to do in a new country is check out their grocery store
their snack section in particular
there’s so many fun snacks I want to try
you need to try this pandan cake
I love anything pandan flavour
these milk tablets also seem to be very popular
there’s also so many coconut products
and also wanted to get some Thai tea
all my shopping done
wanted to try some street snacks as well
I believe these are Khanom buang (Thai crispy pancakes)
it was an interesting mix of sweet and salty
I also got some mango sticky rice to go
for a late night snack
I forgot how good mango sticky rice was
I could eat this every day
we’re starting the day with a 7/11 breakfast
there’s so many things I want to try here
also been loving all the coconut water (cocomax was my favorite)
the packaging is so cute
I got the spinach and cheese grilled sandwich
this was so good
there’s one temple we want to visit, that’s about a 40 min drive
glad we’re finally getting some blue sky
this temple is also well known as the dragon temple
it’s a 17 storey cylindrical tower
with a dragon wrapped around it
this is such a visually stunning temple
you can actually go up the temple through the dragons mouth
this is actually a tunnel through the dragon’s body
it is quite a walk up
and at the top you can see the dragons head up close
this is meant to be the best viewpoint to see Wat Arun temple
think the view will be even better at night, so we’ll come back then
going to kill some time with a massage
this place is the first Thai massage school
by the time we finished it was already dark
at night the temple lights up this golden colour
this bar has a direct view overlooking the temple and water
they mostly do drinks and light snacks
we came around 6 and it was about a 20 minute wait for a balcony seat
but I think it was worth the wait
the atmosphere was nice
with a cool night breeze from the water
starting the day again at the convenience store
these toasts were so good I came back for more
and my coconut water
the convenience store here sells all these vitamins and medicines as well
I heard this was good for dehydration on hot days
these gummies were also good for jetlag
they also have a lot of skincare samples
I heard the the sunscreen in Thailand is meant to be good
we’re getting a coffee before heading off to our first spot
there’s so many coffee options
this was probably one of my favorite coffee shops here
they even gave us some samples
it was so good I got another one for the go
it’s like this coffee cream on coconut water
I think I’m gonna try recreate it at home
the first stop is about a 30 minute drive from the city
the museum has this impressive three-headed elephant statue
assembled from thousands of pieces of copper the statue weighs over 250 tons
it’s even more impressive in person
I also love the pink building design
let’s go have a look inside
the inside is just as impressive
the ceiling is made of stained glass and depicts the universe
the building is divided into 3 storeys
representing the underworld, the human world, and heaven
let’s go up to the top floor
this part is actually in the elephant’s body
you can also find this lovely garden outside
even though the museum is a bit further out
I think it’s totally worth a visit
the fog really adds to the mystical atmosphere
time for another massage
the massage here was really good and they gave us a little gift too
but it was pouring by the time we finished
trying to find a place for dinner
we decided on this hot pot place
perfect for a rainy day
it also came with a Tom Yum broth
it’s really pouring tonight
had to make one last stop to get mango sticky rice
they give you a full mango here
time to head home
looks like another rainy day in Bangkok
I love the clean wooden interior of this cafe
it’s one of those cafes with soft music and a lot of natural light
the citrus and chocolatey flavour really complemented each other
today’s our last day in Bangkok
our flight is not until the afternoon
so we’re just having a chill morning looking around
for lunch we decided to try this famous crab place
I can see why it’s so popular, totally worth it
one last coffee stop
this is a cosy family-run specialty coffee shop
nice little spot to take shelter from the rain
they also had a nice collection of vintage cameras
and the owner gave us complimentary coffee
it was good but definitely a bit over caffeinated
and that brings us to the end of the trip
and we’re off to the next country :)
one last mango sticky rice before I go
